title: Bufnorm crashes on bi-directional drivers in design
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.55+23 (git sha1 e57a2b944, clang++ 11.1.0 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

macOS

### Reproduction Steps

```
read_verilog <<EOF
(*blackbox*)
module foo(inout x);
endmodule
module top();
	wire w;
	foo a(.x(w));
	foo b(.x(w));
endmodule
EOF
bufnorm
```

### Expected Behavior

No crash and valid "bufnormed" representation

### Actual Behavior

```
2. Executing BUFNORM pass (convert to buffer-normalized form).
Buffer-normalizing module top.
  directly driven by cell b port x: w
  directly driven by cell a port x: w
Summary: removed 0, updated 0, kept 0, and created 0 buffers, and updated 0 cell ports.
ERROR: Assert `wire->driverCell_ == nullptr' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:4061.
```
code_blocks:
2. Executing BUFNORM pass (convert to buffer-normalized form).
Buffer-normalizing module top.
  directly driven by cell b port x: w
  directly driven by cell a port x: w
Summary: removed 0, updated 0, kept 0, and created 0 buffers, and updated 0 cell ports.
ERROR: Assert `wire->driverCell_ == nullptr' failed in kernel/rtlil.cc:4061.
